insights.issueTitle: Homepage title may be weak for search visibility
insights.whyItMatters: A title that is missing, too short, or too long can reduce relevance and click-through performance in search results.
insights.recommendedImprovement: Use a title that clearly combines brand, offering, and buyer intent in a concise format.
insights.improvementExample: Example improvement: Das Muller German Shepherds | Puppies, Training, and Structured Placement

insights.issueTitle: Meta description may not be optimized for click-through
insights.whyItMatters: Search users often rely on the description to decide whether to click, so weak length or clarity can lower engagement.
insights.recommendedImprovement: Write a concise description that explains the homepage offer and why a visitor should act.
insights.improvementExample: Example improvement: Purpose-bred German Shepherd puppies, structured placement, and training support for serious homes ready to apply.

insights.issueTitle: CTA language is present but can be made more outcome-focused
insights.whyItMatters: Clear CTA wording helps visitors understand the value of clicking rather than just the action itself.
insights.recommendedImprovement: Refine CTA labels to connect the action with the visitor’s goal.
insights.improvementExample: Example improvement: change a generic CTA to 'Apply for the Right Puppy Match' or 'Start Structured Training'.

insights.issueTitle: Application flow may feel heavy before enough reassurance is established
insights.whyItMatters: If visitors hit a long application flow too early, they may drop off before building confidence in the brand and offer.
insights.recommendedImprovement: Add stronger trust and expectation-setting copy before the application section or break the form into a softer pre-qualification path.
insights.improvementExample: Example improvement: add a short 'What to expect before applying' section above the form.
